english: sgań (sgang) a blister, caused by vesicatories, by long marches etc., C., W.; cf. bsgań (bsgang).
sgań-ba (sgang ba), pf. bsgańs (bsgangs), fut. bsgań (bsgang), to grow or become full Cs.; bud-méd na-so sgań (bud med na so sgang) a marriageable girl.

sgab-pa (sgab pa), secondary form of °gebs-pa ('gebs pa), byá-mas bí-la sgab-pa (bya mas bi la sgab pa) the covering of a young bird by its mother Glr.; gos-sgáb (gos sgab) Lex., skirt or lap of a coat, sgab-túń (sgab thung) a short skirt.

sgam (sgam) chest, box, trunk; sgam-čúń (sgam chung) a little chest or box; sgam-bu (sgam bu) id.; sgam-sgo-máńs (sgam sgo mangs) a chest of drawers, cabinet C.; šiń-sgam (shing sgam) a wooden chest, lčágs-sgam (lcags sgam) an iron chest; kó-sgam (ko sgam) a leather trunk; ró-sgam (ro sgam), resp. spúr-sgam (spur sgam) coffin Cs. — syn. sgrom (sgrom).

sgám-pa (sgam pa), or sgám-po (sgam po) Cs. deep, profound, Sch. also prudent, quiet; Lex. blo-sgam (blo sgam) w.e. Only the following phrase came under my notice: tugs šiń-tu sgám-mo (thugs shin tu sgam mo) he (the prince) is very clever (as a legendary explanation and confirmation of the name sroń-btsan-sgám-po (srong btsan sgam po)). Prob. obsolete.
sgar (sgar) camp, encampment, dmag-sgár (dmag sgar) a military camp, sgar °débs-pa (sgar 'debs pa) to pitch a camp; sgar-míń (sgar ming) C. watch-word, parole, = bso-sgrá (bso sgra).

sgal (sgal) load of a beast of burden, rta-sgal (rta sgal) a horse-load, šiń-rtai sgal (shing rta'i sgal) a cart-load, waggon-load Cs.; sgal °gél-ba (sgal 'gel ba) to put on a load, °páń-ba ('phang ba) to throw it off, °bógs-pa ('bogs pa) to take it off, sgal bsráń-ba (sgal bsrang ba) to adjust or balance it; sgál-rta (sgal rta) pack-horse, sgál-pyugs (sgal phyugs) beast of burden.

sgál-pa (sgal pa) 1. the small of the back, sgál-°dabs (sgal 'dabs) the lumbar region Med. — 2 the croup, crupper, back of a horse Glr.; *gál-pa ton dug* (*gal pa thon dug*) W. the back comes out, i.e. has become sore or galled; sgal-tsigs (sgal tshigs) Med., sgal-rus (sgal rus) col. backbone, spine; sgal-rmá (sgal rma) a sore on an animal's back caused by the load.
sgu-stégs (sgu stegs) Lex. w.e.; Sch. elbow, angle.
sgu-rdó (sgu rdo) a sling Sch.

sgúg-pa (sgug pa), pf. bsgugs (bsgugs), fut. bsgug (bsgug), imp. sgug(s) (sgug(s)), to wait, zlá-ba yčig sgúg-pa (zla ba gcig sgug pa) to wait for a month Glr.; to await, to expect, °či-ba ('chi ba) death Mil.; lám-na sgúg-pa (lam na sgug pa) to wait on the road Mil.; sgúg-čiń sdód-pa (sgug cing sdod pa), W.: *gúg-te dád-če* (*gug te dad ce*) to sit waiting; *i-ru gúg-te dód* (*i ru gug te dod*) W. wait here! sgug-tu °jug-pa (sgug tu 'jug pa) to keep one waiting Glr.; to lie in wait (for a person), to waylay; jág-pas sgúg-pai sa (jag pas sgug pa'i sa) a place where robbers are lying in ambush Mil.; *koń gúg-te dád-če* (*khong gug te dad ce*) W. to bear a grudge, to have a spite against a person.
sgun (sgun) Ld. clap, crack, crash, report (of a gun).
sgud-po (sgud po) father-in-law, sgud-mo (sgud mo) mother-in-law Sch. prov.
sgum-mda (sgum mda') Schr. butt-end of a gun, gun-stock C., W.; spelling dubious.
sgur (sgur) v. dgur (dgur).

sgúl-ba (sgul ba) vb. a. (cf. °gul-ba ('gul ba)), pf. and fut. bsgul (bsgul), to move, agitate, put in motion, rgyud kyań ma sgúl-to (rgyud kyang ma sgul to) he could not even move the bow-string Dzl.; to pull (e.g. the bell-string).
sgeu (sge'u) 1. diminutive of sga (sga), ginger, sgeu-yšér (sge'u gsher) Med., Ssk. आर्द्रक (Hind. adrak), fresh ginger. — 2. a small door.

sgég-pa (sgeg pa) Cs.: to boast, brag; yet not so much with respect to words as to looks and demeanour, so that it may be applied to the airs of coquettish girls (sgég-čiń mdzés-pa (sgeg cing mdzes pa) coquettish Mil., Stg.) as well as to the bearing of insolent youngsters and bullies. sgég-ma (sgeg ma) n. of a goddess; sgég-mo (sgeg mo) Lex. लास्या, a dancing girl.
sgéń-la (sgeng la), or dgeń-la (dgeng la) (?) on, upon Ts.
sger (sger) Sch.: 'different, dissimilar, foreign'. This word I repeatedly met with in books of physical science, without finding the signification given above applicable.

sgo (sgo) 1. door, the aperture itself, as well as the wood-work of the door: sgo °byéd-pa (sgo 'byed pa), W. *pé-če* (*pe ce*), to open the door; *°júg-pa* (*'jug pa*) 1. C. to put in a door, to hang a door on hinges 2. W. to close, to shut the door;
